Research



I. What We’re Looking For…



Insights Into Demographic : 

Who Is Your Customer?

Focusing on freelancers who are getting businesses that are scaling and looking to make more money, bigger tickets and goals. Along with agency owners who have a team to provide services at a higher level. 



For social its usually people who value freedom and control, especially of time and location - lots of family oriented people or travel oriented folks people who can't be bossed around lmao



What Attitudes Do They Have? (Religious, Political, Social, Economic)?



Economics: believe that they can earn more on their own than in a job, belief that economy isn’t necessarily set up in their favor and that they need to take action for the security of their family. 



Companies aren’t hiring full time and moving towards contract workers





What Are Their Hopes and Dreams?



Leaving the 9-5 behind. 

Freedom, independence, more money, being a provider for their families, spending more time with family travel, not living paycheck to paycheck, security (no worried about being laid off). Be your own boss. Not relying one client. Not having to check your bank account constantly. No more kissing bosses ass, no more pointless meetings, no more office politics suck







What Are Their Victories and Failures?



Victories

I've been creating animated videos for the past few years, and honestly it's going pretty good.



Leaving the 9-5 behind. 



Getting a handful of clients



Making more as freelancer than you did in your job



Not having to go back to work after having a kid



Making money as a side hustle. 



Getting a name or reputation…



Wearing comfy yoga pants all day (dressing how you want)



Friends asking what they’re doing right now. 



Not caring about being fired. 







Failures

Getting consistent work but income isn’t increasing. 

I can't increase my monthly income, simply because I can't work more than I already do. (limited bandwidth, or that they are the business) 



Trying to hire someone to help me out didn't work out - I wasted time trying to qualify multiple people, but was never satisfied from the result.



Good evening, hope you're all having a wonderful day. It's been about a month now since I quit my eight to five and started as a freelancer. At first the hype was real and sky was the limit. I mean I like to work from home, wake up when I want, but I feel like I'm missing my coworkers, chit-chat a lot and I'm just so used to having some fun at work. It's weird.



“Office Withdrawal”



The feast famine cycle with freelancers is a REAL problem. Being a beginner freelancer I never know how to balance between too much work and too little work. It's also hard to find work that I enjoy. I'm always laggard on my marketing skills as a freelancer. And finally but not least, the process is so stressful and uncertain if you have minimal experience.



I have been freelancing for a few months now but wanted some advice on the best way to get more clients. I do CAD work and have been for a few years. I don't want to use any websites like Freelancer.com, Upwork.com etc since I have tried these and have spent hours on them with no result. How do you recommend to get consistent clients?





For me, it’s money talks. I suck at charging a set rate confidently and getting clients to see the value of my rate.









What Outside Forces Do THEY Believe Have Prevented Their Best Life?



I can’t scale because there’s only one of me, and nobody does as good of a job as I do. 



I work through a market where my prices are among the highest, and many buyers choose to not work with me due to the prices.



 I think that raising the prices will leave me with much less work, or less working hours but the same income. 



I can’t raise my prices because then nobody would pay me. 



Feast and Famine Cycle



When you register to freelance platforms such as Fiverr or Freelancer, you realize that getting orders is not as easy as you think it would be. Because they are kind of dominated by the already top listed sellers. It makes you think, "why would they pick me instead of them?".





What Are Their Prejudices?



Subcontracting with freelancers overseas is a headache and leads to shoddy work. 



Tons of prejudices against clients - not paying on time, demanding, always wanting to get on calls, not wanting to pay your rates, changing scope of work out of nowhere, spotty communication, they think I’m “on call” for them 24/7. Clients trying to pass off work as quick and easy to devalue your efforts and pay less money to you. 



"Life shouldn't be fit around my working hours, shouldn't have to miss out on my family, watching my kids grow up, because I'm worried about not being able to provide "





Sum Up Their Core Beliefs About Life, Love, and Family In 1-3 Sentences. 











Other Existing Solutions: 

What is the Market Already Using? (List Out)



Hiring out VAs to increase work capacity. 

Getting clients on retainers

Raising Rates (But objections)



Focusing on Repeat Customers



TLDR: In summary, as an amateur freelancer the best thing you can do to get better clients is niche down to a very specific skillset, demonstrate your expertise, cherish your network, and maintain the relationships in them and you'll end up finding work that you'll enjoy doing.





I know I'm a little bit late to the party here, but I have found the best way is like what @Julia_K said. Get out there and spread your name and then get some orders, do good work and get recommended to their friends, etc... This can depend greatly on what area of freelancing you do, but I tend to find this has worked well and I am a writer.



In terms of consistent clients, that's all down to providing good work for anyone you find. Most of my revenue comes from repeat customers, they get comfortable with my services and order from me time and time again. Newer customers are harder to convince as there's so much selection for them and they'll not fully know what USPs they're after, etc..



They’ve just been cold contacting people on LinkedIn. 



What Has Their Experience Been Like?



Personalizing every message takes too long

Don’t know where the lead is at in the sales cycle

Pitching is spammy, don’t like doing it/receiving it

Posting content only gets people near the beginning of the sales cycle - still need to spend more time to nurture and close

Not seeing fast results

Prefer leads coming to them, don’t want to spend more than 30 minutes max per day





What Does the Market Like About Existing Solutions?



What Does the Market Dislike About Existing Solutions?



Upwork and Freelancer

I don't want to use any websites like Freelancer.com, Upwork.com etc since I have tried these and have spent hours on them with no result. How do you recommend to get consistent clients?



I can be considered as a new freelancer too. But as the title of this thread states, finding clients is not an easy task. When you register to freelance platforms such as Fiverr or Freelancer, you realize that getting orders is not as easy as you think it would be. Because they are kind of dominated by the already top listed sellers. It makes you think, "why would they pick me instead of them?". So, in my short freelance 'adventure', I could not find any clients so far. But I am not thinking of giving up yet. Because once you start getting regular orders, it will also cause you to motivate yourself to improve, learn and do more.





Are Their Horror Stories About Existing Solutions?

Does the Market Believe Existing Solution Works?

If Not, Why?



Curiosity:

Has Someone Tried to Solve the Market’s Pain Points Before In A Very Unique Way? 

What Was The Result?

Is There A Conspiratorial Story Behind Why Old Solutions Didn’t Work?

Are There Any Older Attempts to Solve the Problem (Pre-1960) That Are Unique?

What Happened? Were they successful but forgotten? Or were they a failure? Why?



Examples: 

1) Tesla in the energy space. Big energy didn’t want his solutions, he was discredited and shamed. His inventions and discoveries were thrown into the ash heap of history until now.  

2) U.S. Army tried to cure foot fungus during WWII, surgeon general was on a desperate race because troops were missing service time. Finally succeeded by using Undecylenic Acid. But today we forget how effective it is. 





Corruption:

Is There A Belief That the Market’s Pain Point Used To Not Exist, Or Used To Not Be So Bad?

Is There A Belief That It’s Been Recently Exacerbated By Outside Forces?

If So, What Are Those Forces And What’s The Reason Behind Their Presence?



Examples: 

Obesity and diabetes being the result of Dr. Ancil Keyes. 



This isolated group of people doesn’t struggle from whatever condition/pain point that most of us do. In America we DO suffer from this pain point. The reason why is that we are exposed to these outside forces while this isolated group isn’t.
